open_items.title: Confirm the Thursday DCC packet renders in the live app
open_items.summary: Thursday's packet replaced a skeleton file. Open the DCC preview and confirm the schedule, source health, and timeline render correctly.
open_items.notes: This keeps the source-of-truth recovery work from slipping back into skeleton days.

open_items.title: Finish Sweep Suite reader config now that Slack is callable
open_items.summary: Slack searches were callable and returned no hits, but user-context is still empty and keyword alerts are not configured.
open_items.notes: Seed Slack user ID and any keyword alerts before relying on unattended sweeps.

open_items.title: Audit Angel slideshow/export QA
open_items.summary: Carryover from Wednesday: review uncommitted slideshow/export changes, run a light verification pass, then commit or intentionally park.
open_items.notes: Keep this bounded; the goal is decision quality, not a refactor.

open_items.title: HYHH tracker follow-through with backup-first guardrails
open_items.summary: Continue HYHH tracker work only after preserving/exporting browser-local state and confirming blocked-upgrade handling.
open_items.notes: Tuesday's recovery showed the browser database is production state.

notifications.title: Sweep Suite source scan for Thursday
notifications.body: Calendar scanned successfully. Gmail unread/starred found four promotion/newsletter-style messages and no work obligations. Slack connector searches were callable and found no DMs or mentions in the Thursday planning window.

assessment.day_theme: Use the morning social block cleanly, then spend the workday turning yesterday's source recovery into durable routines before evening tennis.
hard_commitments: 07:00-08:45 Social: Coffee Chat at Local Honey Coffee Company
must_do: Confirm Thursday's DCC packet renders instead of a skeleton.
Finish Sweep Suite reader config enough that future Slack/Gmail sweeps are trustworthy.
Review Audit Angel's uncommitted slideshow/export changes and decide commit vs. park.
Continue HYHH only with backup-first guardrails.
nice_to_do: Turn this manual packet shape into a repeatable DCC routine.
Clear or label the four non-actionable Gmail promo/newsletter messages.
risks: The calendar is solid, but Sweep Suite still lacks configured Slack user context and keyword alerts.
Gmail appears noisy and promotion-heavy, so it should not drive the day without filtering.
Evening tennis compresses closeout; protect the 16:00 planning block.

timeline.label: Social: Coffee Chat
timeline.description: Local Honey Coffee Company, 250 Riverplace Unit B, Greenville, SC 29601

timeline.label: Transit / settle
timeline.description: Buffer after coffee before opening the work loop.

timeline.label: DCC packet render check
timeline.description: Open DCC and confirm the Thursday packet is visible, current, and usable before deeper work.
timeline.notes: Goal: make sure DCC is actually carrying today's itinerary, not a stale skeleton. Checklist: open the local DCC preview; confirm the date is 2026-05-14; confirm Coffee Chat and Thursday Tennis appear; confirm the work blocks appear in order from 9:05 through 17:00; check the source-readiness notification; click at least one task card and confirm the detail/notes panel opens. If the app still shows a skeleton or Wednesday data, capture which file/API endpoint it is reading and update the state path before moving on. Done when the live DCC screen matches this packet closely enough to use during the day.

timeline.label: Sweep Suite reader config
timeline.description: Finish enough Sweep Suite configuration that future Slack/Gmail sweeps are trustworthy.
timeline.notes: Goal: turn today's manual connector sweep into a repeatable Sweep Suite run. Checklist: inspect claude-brain/plugins/local/sweep-suite/config/user-context.json; add or confirm the Slack user id if available; decide which keyword_alerts should exist, if any; preserve the current no-alert behavior if no keywords are ready; document that Slack connector searches are now callable; decide whether Gmail should filter promotions/newsletters before becoming a planning source. Done when the config file and notes clearly explain what the next unattended sweep will search and what it will intentionally ignore.

timeline.label: Audit Angel slideshow/export QA
timeline.description: Review Audit Angel's dirty slideshow/export work and choose commit or park.
timeline.notes: Goal: close the loop on the uncommitted Audit Angel export changes without letting it expand into a broad refactor. Checklist: run git status in Repos/audit-angel; review the diff for public/index.html and public/js/export.js; start the app or use the lightest existing verification path; test the slideshow/export flow that changed; note any visual or download regression; either commit with a focused message or write down why the work is parked. Done when the repo state is intentional and there is no mystery dirty work left from the export pass.

timeline.label: Lunch / reset
timeline.description: Keep the afternoon from inheriting morning residue.

timeline.label: HYHH tracker follow-through
timeline.description: Continue HYHH tracker work only after protecting browser-local production state.
timeline.notes: Goal: make progress on HYHH without risking the call tracker data. Checklist: identify the live browser/profile data store being used; export or otherwise preserve the current tracker data before any schema/UI work; confirm blocked-upgrade handling before touching IndexedDB-related code; then choose one narrow follow-through item, preferably artifact-link polish or documentation-upload polish from Tuesday. Avoid opportunistic schema cleanup. Done when the data is backed up, the chosen narrow task is complete or clearly blocked, and any follow-up risk is written down.

timeline.label: DCC packet routine follow-through
timeline.description: Capture today's manual sweep-to-itinerary path as a repeatable DCC routine.
timeline.notes: Goal: make the next itinerary less dependent on memory. Checklist: write down the source order used today: Calendar, Gmail unread/starred, Slack mentions, Slack IM/MPIM search, then DCC carryovers; record the queries and date-window assumptions; decide where that routine belongs in the DCC or Sweep Suite docs/config; note that Gmail found only promotions/newsletters and Slack found no DMs/mentions; add any missing automation or connector gaps as explicit follow-up tasks. Done when a future session can recreate the packet from the written routine without reverse-engineering this chat.

timeline.label: Inbox/admin buffer
timeline.description: Use this flexible block for inbox cleanup, quick replies, or small admin.
timeline.notes: Goal: absorb small communication/admin work without stealing from project blocks. Checklist: scan the four Gmail messages found by the sweep; archive, label, unsubscribe, or leave them if they are intentionally informational; do a quick manual Slack check if anything feels missing from the connector search; handle only replies that take a few minutes; move anything larger into a task instead of doing it here. Done when the inbox noise is lower and no hidden urgent message is nagging at the rest of the day.

timeline.label: Closeout and Friday inputs
timeline.description: Close the loop on actuals and set up Friday before evening tennis.
timeline.notes: Goal: prevent Friday from starting with archaeological work. Checklist: mark completed DCC timeline blocks; mark anything skipped or partially done honestly; record blockers from Sweep Suite, Audit Angel, and HYHH; move unfinished work into Friday or an explicit later bucket; capture any source-confidence issues from today's packet; make sure the evening tennis block remains protected. Done when Friday has clear inputs and today's packet reflects what actually happened.

timeline.label: Dinner / tennis buffer
timeline.description: Travel, food, and gear buffer before tennis.

timeline.label: Thursday Tennis
timeline.description: Calendar note: Southside? Might work from my place or stay in downtown Greenville if preferred.

Gmail sweep is noisy and promotion-heavy
